paragraph:
Velmoria's quiet revolution
One hundred and thirty years on, a scientific (innovation) tests the republic's deepest (conviction)s
LET THIS chapter examine a revolution without (insurrection). One hundred and thirty years after the marshal fell, Velmoria confronts not a tyrant but a technology—a medical (innovation) so profound that it forces the republic to revisit its founding (proposition)s. The breakthrough, a method of organ (transplantation) and cellular (augmentation) that could extend human life by decades, arrives not as a (temptation) to be resisted but as a (disquisition) to be debated.
The (innovation) emerged from the republic's universities, the (fruition) of generations of patient research. Its (presentation) to the public was sober, free of (ostentation) or (misrepresentation). But its (connotation)s were vast. Who would receive the (augmentation)? The wealthy, by simple (imputation) of means? Or all citizens, by the (proposition) of equal right? The (equation) of fairness had no obvious solution, and the republic's deepest (conviction)s were suddenly in (friction) with one another.
A faction urged immediate (authorization) and wide (distribution)—the (abolition) of any (partition) between rich and poor in access to longer life. Their (predilection) was for (centralization): a state monopoly to ensure the (augmentation) reached every citizen without (detraction). Their (conviction), born of the republic's (egalitarian) (disposition), was that to ration life by wealth would be an (infraction) against the founding (proposition) of equality—a kind of (sedition) against the social contract.
Another faction counselled (discretion). They warned that hasty (centralization) and (optimization) might breed (malfunction)—that a state (annexation) of so powerful a technology risked the very (concentration) of power the marshal had craved. Better, they argued, a careful (continuation) of private research under public (sanction), with strict (jurisdiction) over its use. Their (predisposition) was toward (caution): every great (innovation), they noted, carries the (temptation) of (deprivation)—the risk that those who control it will inflict (privation) on those who do not.
Recall how the marshal would have handled such power. He would have seized it by (annexation), made its (distribution) a tool of (domination), and dispensed longer life as a (benediction) to loyalists and a (malediction) to enemies. His every (manifestation) of power had been an (infliction); his every (salutation) a veiled threat. He ruled by (friction) and (vexation), by the (eviction) of dissent and the (infliction) of fear. To him, an (innovation) like this would have been not a public good but an instrument of (subjection)—a means to make his (domination) literally (immortal).
The republic, (chasten)ed by that memory, chose neither hasty (centralization) nor fearful (abolition). It convened a national (disquisition)—a sober (evaluation) of the (innovation)'s (connotation)s, conducted with full public (inspection). Scientists offered their (dissertation)s; ethicists their (disquisition)s; ordinary citizens their (intuition)s. The debate ranged across every (junction) and (disjunction) of the question: the (equation) of cost, the (proposition) of fairness, the (presupposition)s that lay beneath each side's (conviction).
The (interaction) was vigorous but civil. There was (interjection) and (detraction), (vexation) and (friction), but no (insurrection), no (sedition), no (dereliction) of democratic duty. The republic's century-old habit of (deliberation) held. Each (proposition) was tested against the next; each (presupposition) brought into the light of (inspection). The process was slow—an (attrition) of easy answers, a (parturition) of hard wisdom—but it was the republic's own.
The (resolution), when it came, was a fine (equation) of competing goods. The (innovation) would be developed under public (sanction) but not state monopoly; its (distribution) would follow a (proposition) of need, not wealth; an independent body, with clear (jurisdiction), would guard against both (deprivation) of the poor and (centralization) of control. It was no perfect (utopia)—every (partition) of a scarce good leaves someone wanting—but it was a (proposition) the public could accept with (conviction) rather than (compunction).
Beyond the (disquisition), ordinary life proceeded in its (variegated) way. A vintner tended the (fermentation) of the autumn harvest; a baker judged his loaves by (olfaction) and (gustation); a gardener fought the slow (incrustation) of salt on the coastal (terrain). The republic's farmers had reversed the old (deforestation), and new woods softened the hills. There was the steady (continuation) of small joys—a child's first (salutation), a scholar's (valediction) at graduation, the (ovation) for a beloved teacher's last lecture. No (infection) of fear, no (eviction) of hope, troubled the (quotidian) round.
The technology, in time, came to (fruition). Citizens lived longer; the (augmentation) became, by careful (evaluation), a common good rather than a private (predilection). There was, inevitably, some (vexation)—a black market here, an (infraction) there, the occasional (conviction) for fraud. But the republic's (jurisdiction) held; its (detection) of abuse was swift; its (discretion) in enforcement, humane. The (innovation) did not, as the fearful had warned, breed a new (domination). It bred, instead, a quiet (exhilaration)—the (sensation) of a free people who had mastered a great power without being mastered by it.
A populist, of course, tried to make (sedition) of the matter. He railed that the (augmentation) was an (annexation) of nature, a (temptation) toward (decomposition) of the soul, a (misrepresentation) of the republic's values. His (oration)s were a (malediction) dressed as a (benediction); his (aspiration), the (domination) he could not win by (volition) of the voters. He demanded the (abolition) of the technology, the (deportation) of its inventors, the (demolition) of the institutes that made it. But his (superstition) found no (traction). The public, by their own (intuition) and (volition), declined his (proposition).
The (culmination) came not in (ovation) but in (contrition)—a quiet acknowledgement, in the national (forum), that the republic had nearly stumbled. A few had let (infatuation) with the (innovation) outrun their (discretion); others had let fear breed (dereliction). The (valediction) of the debate was a (benediction) on the process itself: that a free people, by patient (deliberation), could face even the (temptation) of immortality without surrendering their (volition) to a strongman's (sanction).
So Velmoria, one hundred and thirty years free, learns that the gravest tests come not only as the (manifestation)s of tyranny but as the (temptation)s of progress—and that the answer to a great (innovation) is neither hasty (centralization) nor fearful (abolition), but the patient (disquisition) of a people governing themselves. The marshal sought (domination) and would have made even longer life an instrument of (subjection); his country, a century and a third past his fall, has chosen the harder (proposition)—to meet each (innovation) with (discretion) rather than (compunction), to guard against both (deprivation) and (centralization), and to find, in the slow (fruition) of honest debate, the surest (conviction) that no technology, however powerful, need ever become a tyrant's (benediction) or a free people's (malediction).
translation:
韦尔莫利亚的静默革命

*历一百三十年，一项科学（创新/innovation）考验着这个共和国最深层的种种（信念/conviction)*

让这一章审视一场没有（暴动起义/insurrection）的革命。元帅倒台一百三十年后，韦尔莫利亚所面对的不是一个暴君，而是一项技术——一项如此深远的医学（创新/innovation)，以至于它迫使这个共和国重新审视它立国的种种（命题/proposition)。这项突破，一种能把人类寿命延长数十年的器官（移植/transplantation）与细胞（增强/augmentation）方法，到来时不是作为一桩需要抵御的（诱惑/temptation)，而是作为一篇需要辩论的（专题论述/disquisition)。

这项（创新/innovation）脱胎于这个共和国的各所大学，是数代人耐心研究的（结成果实/fruition)。它向公众的（展示呈现/presentation）是冷静的，不带（炫耀卖弄/ostentation）或（歪曲误述/misrepresentation)。但它的（含义弦外之音/connotation）是巨大的。谁将获得这（增强/augmentation)？是富人，凭着财力简单的（推断认定/imputation)？还是全体公民，凭着平等权利的（命题/proposition)？公平的（方程式/equation）没有显而易见的解，而这个共和国最深层的种种（信念/conviction），骤然彼此（摩擦抵触/friction）起来。

一个派系力主立即（授权批准/authorization）和广泛（分配/distribution)——废除（abolition）富人与穷人在获得更长寿命上的任何（隔阂分隔/partition)。他们的（偏好/predilection）倾向（中央集权/centralization)：一个国家垄断，以确保这（增强/augmentation）毫无（减损/detraction）地抵达每一个公民。他们的（信念/conviction)，生自这个共和国（平等主义的/egalitarian）（秉性/disposition)，认为按财富来配给生命，将是对平等这一立国（命题/proposition）的一桩（违犯/infraction）——一种对社会契约的（叛乱煽动/sedition)。

另一个派系建议（审慎/discretion)。他们警告说，仓促的（中央集权/centralization）和（最优化/optimization）可能滋生（故障失灵/malfunction)——一个国家对如此强大之技术的（兼并接管/annexation)，恰恰冒着元帅曾经渴求的那种权力（集中/concentration）之险。他们主张，不如让私人研究在公共（许可/sanction）之下审慎地（延续/continuation)，对其使用施以严格的（管辖权/jurisdiction)。他们的（倾向性/predisposition）偏向（谨慎/caution)：他们指出，每一项伟大的（创新/innovation)，都携带着（剥夺/deprivation）的（诱惑/temptation)——即掌控它的人会对不掌控它的人施加（匮乏困苦/privation）的风险。

回想元帅当年会如何处置这样一种力量。他会以（兼并接管/annexation）攫取它、把它的（分配/distribution）变成（支配统治/domination）的工具、并把更长的寿命作为给死忠的一份（祝福/benediction）、给敌人的一句（诅咒/malediction）来派发。他权力的每一次（显现/manifestation）都是一次（施加折磨/infliction)；他每一句（致意问候/salutation）都是一个掩饰着的威胁。他靠（摩擦倾轧/friction）和（烦扰/vexation）来统治，靠对异见的（驱逐逐出/eviction）和对恐惧的（施加/infliction)。在他看来，一项这样的（创新/innovation）将不是一项公共福祉，而是一件（征服压制/subjection）的工具——一种让他的（支配统治/domination）真正（不朽/immortal）的手段。

这个共和国，被那段记忆所（惩戒警醒/chasten)，既不选仓促的（中央集权/centralization)，也不选惶恐的（废除/abolition)。它召开了一场全国性的（专题论述/disquisition)——一场对这项（创新/innovation）之（含义/connotation）的冷静（评估/evaluation)，在全面的公众（检视/inspection）之下进行。科学家们奉上他们的（论文专论/dissertation)；伦理学家们奉上他们的（专题论述/disquisition)；普通公民们奉上他们的（直觉/intuition)。辩论横跨了这个问题的每一个（接合点/junction）与（分离点/disjunction)：成本的（方程式/equation)、公平的（命题/proposition)、潜藏在各方（信念/conviction）之下的种种（预设前提/presupposition)。

这场（互动交锋/interaction）激烈却文明。其间有（插话打断/interjection）和（贬损减损/detraction)、（烦扰/vexation）和（摩擦/friction)，但没有（暴动起义/insurrection)、没有（叛乱煽动/sedition)、没有对民主职责的（玩忽懈怠/dereliction)。这个共和国百年来的（审议/deliberation）习惯守住了。每一个（命题/proposition）都对照下一个来检验；每一个（预设/presupposition）都被带到（检视/inspection）的光亮之下。这个过程是缓慢的——一场轻巧答案的（消磨损耗/attrition)、一次艰难智慧的（分娩诞生/parturition)——但它是这个共和国自己的。

那（决议解决/resolution）到来时，是一道相互竞争之善的精妙（方程式/equation)。这项（创新/innovation）将在公共（许可/sanction）之下、而非国家垄断之下来开发；它的（分配/distribution）将遵循一个需要的（命题/proposition)、而非财富；一个具有明确（管辖权/jurisdiction）的独立机构，将既防范对穷人的（剥夺/deprivation)、也防范控制权的（集中/centralization)。它不是一个完美的（乌托邦/utopia)——一种稀缺之善的每一次（分隔/partition）都会留下某个想要而不得的人——但它是一个公众能够带着（信念/conviction）、而非（愧疚踌躇/compunction）来接受的（命题/proposition)。

这场（专题论述/disquisition）之外，寻常生活以它（多姿多样的/variegated）方式推进着。一个酿酒师照料着秋收的（发酵/fermentation)；一个面包师靠（嗅觉/olfaction）和（味觉/gustation）来评判他的面包；一个园丁与海岸（地形/terrain）上盐分缓慢的（结壳/incrustation）作斗争。这个共和国的农夫们已经扭转了旧日的（森林砍伐/deforestation)，新的林子柔化了山丘。其间有小小欢乐稳定的（延续/continuation)——一个孩子第一声（问候致意/salutation)、一个学者毕业时的（告别辞/valediction)、为一位受爱戴老师最后一课而起的（起立鼓掌/ovation)。没有恐惧的（感染/infection)、没有希望的（驱逐/eviction)，来搅扰这（日常的/quotidian）常轨。

这项技术，假以时日，（结成果实/fruition）了。公民们活得更久了；那（增强/augmentation)，经由审慎的（评估/evaluation)，成了一项公共福祉、而非一种私人（偏好/predilection)。不可避免地，其间有一些（烦扰/vexation)——这边一个黑市、那边一桩（违犯/infraction)、偶尔一次对欺诈的（定罪/conviction)。但这个共和国的（管辖权/jurisdiction）守住了；它对滥用的（侦查侦测/detection）迅速；它在执法上的（审慎裁量/discretion)，人道宽厚。这项（创新/innovation）并没有像惶恐者所警告的那样，滋生出一种新的（支配统治/domination)。它反倒滋生出一种静默的（振奋欢欣/exhilaration)——一群驾驭了一种伟大力量、却没有被它所驾驭的自由人民那份（感受/sensation)。

一个民粹主义者，当然，企图把这件事弄成（叛乱煽动/sedition)。他怒斥这（增强/augmentation）是对自然的（兼并僭夺/annexation)、是走向灵魂（腐坏分解/decomposition）的一桩（诱惑/temptation)、是对这个共和国价值观的（歪曲误述/misrepresentation)。他的（演说/oration）是乔装成（祝福/benediction）的（诅咒/malediction)；他的（渴望追求/aspiration)，是他无法靠选民的（意志选择/volition）赢得的（支配统治/domination)。他要求（废除/abolition）这项技术、（驱逐流放/deportation）它的发明者、（拆毁/demolition）造出它的那些研究院。但他的（迷信妄信/superstition）找不到（牵引立足点/traction)。公众，凭着他们自己的（直觉/intuition）和（意志选择/volition)，拒绝了他的（命题/proposition)。

那（顶点高潮/culmination）到来时，不是在（起立鼓掌/ovation）之中，而是在（痛悔自省/contrition）之中——一种静默的承认，在全国性的（论坛/forum）里承认：这个共和国曾险些失足。少数几个人曾让对这项（创新/innovation）的（痴迷/infatuation）跑过了他们的（审慎/discretion)；另一些人则曾让恐惧滋生（玩忽懈怠/dereliction)。这场辩论的（告别辞/valediction)，是对这个过程本身的一份（祝福/benediction)：一个自由的民族，凭着耐心的（审议/deliberation)，能够面对哪怕是不朽的（诱惑/temptation)，而不把他们的（意志选择/volition）交给一个强人的（许可/sanction)。

于是韦尔莫利亚，自由了一百三十载，领悟到：最严峻的考验不只作为暴政的（显现/manifestation）而来，也作为进步的（诱惑/temptation）而来——而对一项伟大（创新/innovation）的回答，既不是仓促的（中央集权/centralization)，也不是惶恐的（废除/abolition)，而是一群自我治理之人民耐心的（专题论述/disquisition)。元帅追求（支配统治/domination)，会把哪怕是更长的寿命也变成一件（征服压制/subjection）的工具；他的国家，在他倒台一个世纪又三分之一之后，选择了那更艰难的（命题/proposition)——以（审慎/discretion）、而非（愧疚踌躇/compunction）去迎接每一项（创新/innovation)、既防范（剥夺/deprivation）也防范（集中/centralization)、并在诚实辩论缓慢的（结成果实/fruition）中，找到那最确凿的（信念/conviction)：没有哪项技术，无论多么强大，需要变成一个暴君的（祝福/benediction）、或一个自由民族的（诅咒/malediction)。
text:
本批词根亮点：

• -cracy 大家族：democracy/aristocracy/autocracy/theocracy/plutocracy/bureaucracy/gerontocracy，全来自希腊 kratos（统治）

• tragedy：来自希腊语"山羊之歌"（tragos+ode），可能因戏剧奖品是山羊

• toady：来自江湖郎中的助手，假装吞食有毒蟾蜍（toad），后演变为"马屁精"

• conspiracy：con（共同）+ spir（呼吸），共同呼吸秘密=阴谋，inspire/spirit/aspire 同根

• parley/parliament/parole：全来自法语 parler（说话）

• redundancy：re（再）+ unda（波），浪潮一再涌来=多余，inundate（淹没）同根

• jeopardy：来自法语"均等的游戏"（jeu parti），表示五五开的危险赌局

quiz.question: _____ transplantation
quiz.explanation: 移植, 器官移植. The patient needed urgent organ transplantation. 患者需要紧急器官移植。 | trans->跨越，plant->种植，transplantation->移种到新处=移植（cf. implant植入，plantation种植园）

quiz.question: ______ augmentation
quiz.explanation: 增加, 预算增加. The department requested budget augmentation. 该部门要求预算增加。 | aug->增加（拉丁augere），augmentation->增加（cf. augur占卜=观察鸟群增减，August八月=大帝之月）

quiz.question: ____ fermentation
quiz.explanation: 发酵, 葡萄酒发酵. Wine fermentation takes several weeks. 葡萄酒发酵需要几周时间。 | ferment->沸腾（拉丁fervere），fermentation->发热沸腾=发酵（cf. fervent热情的，fever发烧）

quiz.question: _____ presentation
quiz.explanation: 演示, 销售演示. The team prepared a compelling sales presentation. 团队准备了一个引人注目的销售演示。 | pre->前，sent->放置，presentation->放在人前=展示（cf. present呈现，represent代表）

quiz.question: __________ misrepresentation
quiz.explanation: 歪曲, 欺诈性歪曲. The lawsuit alleged fraudulent misrepresentation. 诉讼指控欺诈性歪曲。 | mis->错误，re->再，present->展示，misrepresentation->错误展示=歪曲（cf. represent代表）

quiz.question: ____________ ostentation
quiz.explanation: 炫耀, 炫耀. His wealth was displayed with ostentatious ostentation. 他的财富以炫耀的方式展示。 | os->面前，tent->伸展（拉丁ostendere），ostentation->在人前伸展=炫耀（cf. ostentatious炫耀的，ostensibly表面上）

quiz.question: ____ connotation
quiz.explanation: 含义, 词语含义. The word connotation varies by culture. 词语含义因文化而异。 | con->共同，not->标记（拉丁nota），connotation->附加标记的意思=含义（cf. note笔记，denote表示，annotate注释）

quiz.question: ______ potation
quiz.explanation: 饮酒, 烈酒. He indulged in strong potation at the tavern. 他在酒馆沉溺于烈酒。 | pot->喝（拉丁potare），potation->饮酒（cf. potion药水，poison毒药，symposium共饮讨论会）

quiz.question: _____ __ temptation
quiz.explanation: 诱惑, 屈服于诱惑. Don't yield to temptation to cheat. 不要屈服于作弊的诱惑。 | tempt->试探（拉丁temptare），temptation->试探引诱=诱惑（cf. attempt尝试，contempt蔑视）

quiz.question: ________ dissertation
quiz.explanation: 论文, 博士论文. She defended her doctoral dissertation successfully. 她成功答辩了博士论文。 | dis->分开，sert->连接（拉丁serere），dissertation->分开连接各论点=详细论述/论文（cf. assert断言，insert插入）

quiz.question: ____ deportation
quiz.explanation: 驱逐出境, 大规模驱逐出境. The regime ordered mass deportation of dissidents. 政权下令大规模驱逐持不同政见者。 | de->离开，port->携带（拉丁portare），deportation->强制带离=驱逐出境（cf. import进口，export出口，transport运输）

quiz.question: _____ manifestation
quiz.explanation: 表现, 明显的表现. The symptoms are a clear manifestation of disease. 这些症状是疾病的明显表现。 | mani->手，fest->显现（拉丁），manifestation->被手握住显现=表现（cf. manifest明显的，infest侵扰）

quiz.question: __________ deforestation
quiz.explanation: 森林砍伐, 雨林砍伐. Rainforest deforestation threatens biodiversity. 雨林砍伐威胁生物多样性。 | de->去除，forest->森林，deforestation->去除森林=砍伐（cf. reforestation重新造林）

quiz.question: _____ __ gustation
quiz.explanation: 味觉, 味觉. The illness affected her sense of gustation. 疾病影响了她的味觉。 | gust->品尝（拉丁gustare），gustation->品尝的感觉=味觉（cf. disgust厌恶=味道令人作呕，gusto热情）

quiz.question: _______ incrustation
quiz.explanation: 结壳, 钙结壳. The pipes showed calcium incrustation buildup. 管道显示出钙结壳积聚。 | in->在上，crust->外壳，incrustation->在上面结硬壳=结壳（cf. crust面包皮，encrust覆以硬壳）

quiz.question: ____ salutation
quiz.explanation: 问候, 热情的问候. He greeted us with a warm salutation. 他用热情的问候迎接我们。 | salut->健康/平安（拉丁salus），salutation->祝平安=问候（cf. salute敬礼，salutary有益的）

quiz.question: ____ reputation
quiz.explanation: 声誉, 良好的声誉. The company maintained a good reputation. 公司保持了良好的声誉。 | re->反复，put->思考（拉丁putare），reputation->被反复思考评价=声誉（cf. repute名声，dispute争论，compute计算）

quiz.question: _____ imputation
quiz.explanation: 归咎, 虚假的归咎. He rejected the false imputation of guilt. 他拒绝了对罪行的虚假归咎。 | im->朝向，put->思考/计算，imputation->把过失算在某人头上=归咎（cf. compute计算，putative推定的）

quiz.question: ___________ evaluation
quiz.explanation: 评估, 绩效评估. Employees receive annual performance evaluation. 员工接受年度绩效评估。 | e->向外，val->价值（拉丁valere），evaluation->算出价值=评估（cf. value价值，valid有效的，prevail胜出）

quiz.question: ______ continuation
quiz.explanation: 继续, 系列继续. Fans eagerly awaited the series continuation. 粉丝急切地等待系列继续。 | con->共同，tinu->持续（拉丁tenere->持有），continuation->持续保持=继续（cf. contain容纳，retain保留）

quiz.question: _____ ___ equation
quiz.explanation: 方程, 解方程. Students learned to solve the equation. 学生学会了解方程。 | equ->相等（拉丁aequus），equation->使两边相等的式子=方程（cf. equal平等，equator赤道，adequate足够的）

quiz.question: _____ infatuation
quiz.explanation: 迷恋, 短暂的迷恋. His attraction was merely a brief infatuation. 他的吸引只是短暂的迷恋。 | in->使，fatu->愚蠢（拉丁fatuus），infatuation->被愚弄的迷恋=迷恋（cf. fatuous愚蠢的，fatuity愚蠢）

quiz.question: ____ derivation
quiz.explanation: 派生, 词源派生. The dictionary explains word derivation. 字典解释词源派生。 | de->离开，riv->流（拉丁rivus->河流），derivation->从源头流出=派生（cf. derive获取，river河流）

quiz.question: privation ___ ____
quiz.explanation: 贫困, 贫困与匮乏. The refugees endured privation and want. 难民忍受贫困与匮乏。 | priv->剥夺（拉丁privare），privation->被剥夺必需品=贫困（cf. deprive剥夺，private私人的=被隔离的）

quiz.question: _____ deprivation
quiz.explanation: 剥夺, 睡眠剥夺. Sleep deprivation affects health negatively. 睡眠剥夺对健康有负面影响。 | de->彻底，priv->剥夺，deprivation->完全剥夺（cf. privation贫困，private私人的）

quiz.question: ________ ovation
quiz.explanation: 热烈鼓掌, 起立鼓掌. The performance received a standing ovation. 表演获得了起立鼓掌。 | ovatio->小凯旋式（拉丁，比triumphal低一级），ovation->热烈欢呼（cf. triumph凯旋）

quiz.question: ____ innovation
quiz.explanation: 创新, 技术创新. Silicon Valley drives tech innovation. 硅谷推动技术创新。 | in->进入，nov->新（拉丁novus），innovation->引入新东西=创新（cf. novel小说/新颖的，renovate翻新）

quiz.question: relaxation ____
quiz.explanation: 放松, 放松时间. Everyone needs relaxation time. 每个人都需要放松时间。 | re->再次，lax->松弛（拉丁laxus），relaxation->重新放松（cf. lax松懈的，laxative泻药，release释放）

quiz.question: ___________ annexation
quiz.explanation: 吞并, 领土吞并. The war resulted in territorial annexation. 战争导致了领土吞并。 | an->朝向，nex->绑（拉丁nectere），annexation->绑到自己身边=吞并（cf. connect连接，annex附属建筑）

quiz.question: _____ vexation
quiz.explanation: 烦恼, 日常烦恼. She dealt with daily vexation calmly. 她平静地处理日常烦恼。 | vex->摇晃/折腾（拉丁vexare），vexation->被折腾=烦恼（cf. vexing令人烦恼的，vex恼怒）

quiz.question: ______________ centralization
quiz.explanation: 集中化, 行政集中化. The reform increased administrative centralization. 改革增加了行政集中化。 | centr->中心，ize->使成为，centralization->使权力集中=集中化（cf. center中心，eccentric偏心的）

quiz.question: ____ optimization
quiz.explanation: 优化, 代码优化. Developers focused on code optimization. 开发人员专注于代码优化。 | optim->最好（拉丁optimus），optimization->使达到最佳=优化（cf. optimism乐观主义，optimum最佳状态）

quiz.question: characterization ___
quiz.explanation: 人物塑造, 人物塑造弧. The novel featured strong characterization arc. 小说有很强的人物塑造弧。 | character->性格（希腊kharakter->雕刻的标记），characterization->刻画性格=人物塑造

quiz.question: _______ authorization
quiz.explanation: 授权, 书面授权. The transaction requires written authorization. 交易需要书面授权。 | author->权威/创作者，ize->使成为，authorization->赋予权力=授权（cf. authority权威，authorize授权）

quiz.question: vaporization ____
quiz.explanation: 汽化, 汽化率. Temperature affects vaporization rate. 温度影响汽化率。 | vapor->蒸气（拉丁vapor），vaporization->变成蒸气=汽化（cf. evaporate蒸发，vapor trail飞机云迹）

quiz.question: ________ sensitization
quiz.explanation: 致敏, 过敏原致敏. Exposure causes allergen sensitization. 暴露导致过敏原致敏。 | sens->感觉，ize->使成为，sensitization->使对某物敏感=致敏（cf. sense感觉，sensitive敏感的）

quiz.question: _____ faction
quiz.explanation: 派系, 党内派系. A radical party faction emerged. 一个激进的党内派系出现了。 | fac->制造/做（拉丁facere），faction->一起行动的小集团=派系（cf. fact事实，manufacture制造，satisfy满足）

quiz.question: ____ rarefaction
quiz.explanation: 稀疏, 波的稀疏. Sound waves create wave rarefaction. 声波产生波的稀疏。 | rare->稀少，fac->制造，rarefaction->使变稀疏（cf. rarefy稀释，rare稀有的/半熟的）

quiz.question: _____ __ olfaction
quiz.explanation: 嗅觉, 嗅觉. Dogs have an acute sense of olfaction. 狗有敏锐的嗅觉。 | olf->嗅（拉丁olfacere->用鼻子感知），olfaction->嗅觉（cf. olfactory嗅觉的）

quiz.question: ______ interaction
quiz.explanation: 互动, 社交互动. Children learn through social interaction. 儿童通过社交互动学习。 | inter->彼此之间，act->行动，interaction->相互之间的行动=互动（cf. react反应，transact处理）

quiz.question: ______ fraction
quiz.explanation: 分数, 真分数. A proper fraction is less than one. 真分数小于一。 | fract->打破（拉丁frangere），fraction->打破整体的一部分=分数（cf. fracture骨折，fragment碎片，refract折射）

quiz.question: _____ refraction
quiz.explanation: 折射, 光折射. Prisms demonstrate light refraction. 棱镜展示光折射。 | re->回，fract->破/弯折，refraction->弯折回来=折射（cf. fraction分数，fracture骨折，diffraction衍射）

quiz.question: _______ infraction
quiz.explanation: 违规, 交通违规. He received a fine for traffic infraction. 他因交通违规收到罚款。 | in->破坏，fract->破，infraction->破坏规则=违规（cf. fraction分数，fracture骨折，infringe侵犯）

quiz.question: ______ detraction
quiz.explanation: 贬损, 不公平的贬损. His success suffered unfair detraction. 他的成功遭受不公平的贬损。 | de->向下，tract->拉，detraction->向下拉扯=贬损（cf. attract吸引，distract分散，subtract减去）

quiz.question: ____ affection
quiz.explanation: 感情, 表现感情. Parents show affection to their children. 父母对孩子表现感情。 | af->朝向，fect->做/影响，affection->朝向某人的情感=感情（cf. affect影响，effect效果，infect感染）

quiz.question: _________ infection
quiz.explanation: 感染, 细菌感染. Antibiotics treat bacterial infection. 抗生素治疗细菌感染。 | in->进入，fect->做/腐坏，infection->坏东西进入体内=感染（cf. disinfect消毒，infect感染，defect缺陷）

quiz.question: ___ _______ injection
quiz.explanation: 注射, 流感疫苗注射. She received her annual flu vaccine injection. 她接受了年度流感疫苗注射。 | in->进入，ject->抛/注入，injection->注入=注射（cf. eject弹出，project投影，reject拒绝）

quiz.question: _____ projection
quiz.explanation: 预测, 销售预测. The company revised its sales projection. 公司修改了销售预测。 | pro->向前，ject->抛，projection->向前抛出=预测/投影（cf. inject注射，eject弹出，subject主题）

quiz.question: ______ interjection
quiz.explanation: 插话, 突然的插话. His sudden interjection surprised everyone. 他突然的插话让所有人惊讶。 | inter->之间，ject->抛，interjection->抛入两者之间=插话/感叹词（cf. inject注射，project投影）

quiz.question: predilection ___
quiz.explanation: 偏好, 偏好于. She has a predilection for classical music. 她对古典音乐有偏好。 | pre->提前，dilect->选择/爱（拉丁diligere），predilection->预先选择喜爱=偏好（cf. elect选举，select选择）

quiz.question: _____ collection
quiz.explanation: 收藏, 邮票收藏. His stamp collection is quite valuable. 他的邮票收藏非常有价值。 | col->共同，lect->收集，collection->共同收集=收藏（cf. elect选举，select精选，lecture演讲=选读）

quiz.question: ______ inspection
quiz.explanation: 检查, 安全检查. Buildings undergo regular safety inspection. 建筑物定期接受安全检查。 | in->进入，spect->看，inspection->进入仔细查看=检查（cf. expect期待，respect尊重，suspect怀疑）

quiz.question: _____ insurrection
quiz.explanation: 叛乱, 武装叛乱. The military crushed the armed insurrection. 军队镇压了武装叛乱。 | in->向上，surrect->起身（拉丁surgere），insurrection->起身反抗=叛乱（cf. resurrection复活，surge汹涌）

quiz.question: ____ dissection
quiz.explanation: 解剖, 青蛙解剖. Biology students performed frog dissection. 生物学学生进行了青蛙解剖。 | dis->分开，sect->切，dissection->切开分开=解剖（cf. section截面，bisect二等分，sector扇形）

quiz.question: _____ detection
quiz.explanation: 检测, 欺诈检测. Banks use algorithms for fraud detection. 银行使用算法进行欺诈检测。 | de->去除，tect->遮盖，detection->去除遮盖=检测/发现（cf. protect保护=提前遮盖，detect侦测）

quiz.question: _____ _ malediction
quiz.explanation: 诅咒, 说诅咒. The witch would speak a malediction. 女巫会说诅咒。 | male->坏，dict->说，malediction->说坏话=诅咒（cf. benediction祝福，predict预言，dictate命令）

quiz.question: ________ valediction
quiz.explanation: 告别辞, 告别辞. The speaker offered a farewell valediction. 演讲者献上了告别辞。 | vale->再见（拉丁valere->保重），dict->说，valediction->说保重=告别辞（cf. valid有效的，value价值）

quiz.question: ____ benediction
quiz.explanation: 祝福, 给予祝福. The priest would give benediction to worshippers. 神父会给信徒祝福。 | bene->好，dict->说，benediction->说好话=祝福（cf. benefit利益，benevolent仁慈的，malediction诅咒）

quiz.question: _____ jurisdiction
quiz.explanation: 管辖权, 法院管辖权. The case fell under court jurisdiction. 该案件属于法院管辖权。 | juris->法律（拉丁jus），dict->说，jurisdiction->法律说了算的地方=管辖权（cf. jury陪审团，justice正义）

quiz.question: dereliction __ ____
quiz.explanation: 失职, 失职. The officer was charged with dereliction of duty. 该军官被指控失职。 | de->彻底，relinqu->放弃（拉丁），dereliction->彻底放弃=失职/废弃（cf. relinquish放弃，relic遗物）

quiz.question: _______ infliction
quiz.explanation: 施加, 痛苦的施加. The punishment was a painful infliction. 惩罚是痛苦的施加. | in->朝向，flict->打击（拉丁fligere），infliction->打击加诸=施加（cf. conflict冲突，afflict折磨）

quiz.question: ______ friction
quiz.explanation: 摩擦, 社会摩擦. The policy caused social friction. 该政策引起了社会摩擦。 | frict->摩擦（拉丁fricare），friction->摩擦（cf. fray磨损，affront冒犯=当面摩擦）

quiz.question: ______ eviction
quiz.explanation: 驱逐, 强制驱逐. Tenants faced forced eviction. 租户面临强制驱逐。 | e->向外，vict->征服（拉丁vincere），eviction->征服驱出=驱逐（cf. victory胜利，convict定罪，convince说服）

quiz.question: ________ conviction
quiz.explanation: 定罪, 刑事定罪. The trial resulted in criminal conviction. 审判导致了刑事定罪。 | con->完全，vict->征服，conviction->完全征服=定罪/坚定信念（cf. convince说服，evict驱逐）

quiz.question: ____ sanction
quiz.explanation: 制裁, 寻求制裁. Nations agreed to seek sanction. 各国同意寻求制裁。 | sanct->神圣/批准（拉丁sanctus），sanction->官方批准=制裁/认可（一词两义，cf. sacred神圣，sanctuary圣所）

quiz.question: ____ distinction
quiz.explanation: 荣誉, 高荣誉. She graduated with high distinction. 她以高荣誉毕业。 | dis->分开，stinct->刺/标记，distinction->区分标记=区别/荣誉（cf. distinct明显的，extinct绝种的，instinct本能）

quiz.question: ______ malfunction
quiz.explanation: 故障, 系统故障. A system malfunction caused delays. 系统故障导致延误。 | mal->坏，funct->运作，malfunction->运作不好=故障（cf. function功能，dysfunction功能障碍）

quiz.question: ____ junction
quiz.explanation: 交叉口, 道路交叉口. Turn left at the road junction. 在道路交叉口左转。 | junct->连接（拉丁jungere），junction->连接点=交叉口（cf. conjunction连接，injunction禁令，adjunct附属）

quiz.question: _____ injunction
quiz.explanation: 禁令, 法院禁令. The judge issued a court injunction. 法官发布了法院禁令。 | in->进入，junct->命令/连接，injunction->命令进入=禁令（cf. junction交叉口，conjunction连词）

quiz.question: conjunction __ ______
quiz.explanation: 结合, 事件的结合. The success was a conjunction of events. 成功是事件的结合。 | con->共同，junct->连接，conjunction->共同连接=结合/连词（cf. junction交叉口，disjunction分离）

quiz.question: _______ disjunction
quiz.explanation: 分离, 逻辑分离. The argument showed logical disjunction. 论证显示出逻辑分离。 | dis->分开，junct->连接，disjunction->断开连接=分离（cf. conjunction结合，junction交叉口）

quiz.question: _____ __ compunction
quiz.explanation: 内疚, 内疚的刺痛. He felt no sting of compunction. 他没有感到内疚的刺痛。 | com->加强，punct->刺（拉丁pungere），compunction->良心被刺痛=内疚（cf. puncture刺穿，punctual准时=刺中时间点）

quiz.question: ________ induction
quiz.explanation: 感应, 磁感应. The motor uses magnetic induction. 电机使用磁感应。 | in->进入，duct->引导，induction->引导进入=感应/归纳（cf. deduction演绎，conduct指挥，educate教育）

quiz.question: _______ obstruction
quiz.explanation: 阻塞, 交通阻塞. The accident caused traffic obstruction. 事故造成了交通阻塞。 | ob->对抗，struct->建造，obstruction->建造障碍=阻塞（cf. construct建造，destroy摧毁，instruct指导）

quiz.question: accretion ____
quiz.explanation: 吸积, 吸积盘. Black holes have an accretion disk. 黑洞有吸积盘。 | ac->朝向，cret->生长（拉丁crescere），accretion->朝向生长积累=吸积（cf. concrete具体的，increase增加，crescent月牙）

quiz.question: ________ discretion
quiz.explanation: 判断力, 运用判断力. Judges must exercise discretion. 法官必须运用判断力。 | dis->分开，cret->筛选（拉丁cernere），discretion->分开筛选=判断力（cf. discern辨别，discreet谨慎的，secret秘密）

quiz.question: __ addition
quiz.explanation: 此外, 此外. In addition, we need more funding. 此外,我们需要更多资金。 | ad->朝向，dit->给（拉丁dare），addition->额外给予=添加/此外（cf. data数据，donate捐赠，tradition传统）

quiz.question: ______ sedition
quiz.explanation: 煽动叛乱, 煽动叛乱. The rebel leaders were charged with commit sedition. 叛乱领导人被控煽动叛乱。 | sed->分开/旁边，it->走（拉丁），sedition->走到旁边煽动=煽动叛乱（cf. separate分开，circuit绕行）

quiz.question: _________ coalition
quiz.explanation: 联盟, 政治联盟. Several parties formed a political coalition. 几个政党组成了政治联盟。 | co->共同，alit->结合（拉丁alescere->成长），coalition->共同成长结合=联盟（cf. coalesce合并）

quiz.question: abolition ________
quiz.explanation: 废除, 废除运动. The abolition movement fought against slavery. 废除运动反对奴隶制。 | ab->离开，ol->成长（拉丁），abolition->使停止成长=废除（cf. abolish废除，adolescent青少年=正在成长的）

quiz.question: ________ demolition
quiz.explanation: 拆除, 建筑拆除. The building demolition took weeks. 建筑拆除花了数周时间。 | de->向下，mol->大块/建造（拉丁moles），demolition->把建造物推倒=拆除（cf. molecule分子，mole痣）

quiz.question: ____ volition
quiz.explanation: 意志, 自由意志. He acted of his own free volition. 他出于自己的自由意志行事。 | vol->意志（拉丁volo->我想要），volition->意志（cf. volunteer自愿者，benevolent仁慈的=好意志）

quiz.question: _____ munitions
quiz.explanation: 军火, 储存军火. The army needed to store munitions safely. 军队需要安全储存军火。 | munit->防御工事（拉丁munire），munitions->防御用的武器弹药=军火（cf. ammunition弹药，immunize免疫）

quiz.question: _________ contrition
quiz.explanation: 悔恨, 真诚的悔恨. He expressed heartfelt contrition for his actions. 他对自己的行为表达了真诚的悔恨。 | con->加强，trit->磨碎（拉丁terere），contrition->被良心磨碎=悔恨（cf. trite陈腐的，attrition磨损）

quiz.question: _______ attrition
quiz.explanation: 消耗, 自然消耗. Staff declined through natural attrition. 员工通过自然消耗减少。 | at->朝向，trit->磨损，attrition->持续磨损消耗（cf. contrition悔恨，trite陈腐的=被磨光了的）

quiz.question: _____ nutrition
quiz.explanation: 营养, 儿童营养. Proper child nutrition is essential. 适当的儿童营养至关重要。 | nutri->滋养（拉丁nutrire），nutrition->滋养的过程=营养（cf. nurture培育，nurse护士，nourish滋养）

quiz.question: _______ parturition
quiz.explanation: 分娩, 自然分娩. The mother chose natural parturition. 母亲选择了自然分娩。 | partur->分娩（拉丁parere->生产），parturition->分娩过程（cf. parent父母，postpartum产后的）

quiz.question: _________ disquisition
quiz.explanation: 专题论文, 学术专题论文. The professor wrote a scholarly disquisition. 教授写了一篇学术专题论文。 | dis->深入，quis->追问（拉丁quaerere），disquisition->深入追问研究=专题论文（cf. inquisition审问，quisite必要的）

quiz.question: _______ deposition
quiz.explanation: 证词, 证人证词. The lawyer took the witness deposition. 律师采取了证人证词。 | de->向下，pos->放置，deposition->放下陈述=证词/废黜（cf. deposit存款，compose作曲，impose施加）

quiz.question: ______ preposition
quiz.explanation: 介词, 介词宾语. The noun is an object preposition. 这个名词是介词宾语。 | pre->之前，pos->放置，preposition->放在名词前面的词=介词（cf. position位置，disposition性情，composition作品）

quiz.question: _______ decomposition
quiz.explanation: 分解, 有机分解. Bacteria aid organic decomposition. 细菌帮助有机分解。 | de->解除，com->共同，pos->放置，decomposition->把组合拆开=分解（cf. compose作曲，composition作品）

quiz.question: ____ _ proposition
quiz.explanation: 提议, 提出提议. The investor will make a proposition. 投资者将提出提议。 | pro->向前，pos->放置，proposition->向前放出的提案=提议（cf. propose求婚，dispose处置，expose暴露）

quiz.question: ______ presupposition
quiz.explanation: 预设, 隐藏的预设. The argument contains a hidden presupposition. 论证包含隐藏的预设。 | pre->提前，sup->在下，pos->放置，presupposition->提前放在下面的假设=预设（cf. suppose假设，supposition猜测）

quiz.question: _____ disposition
quiz.explanation: 性情, 开朗的性情. She has a naturally sunny disposition. 她天生有开朗的性情。 | dis->分开，pos->放置，disposition->各部分的排列方式=性情/处置（cf. compose组合，deposit存放）

quiz.question: _______ predisposition
quiz.explanation: 倾向, 遗传倾向. Some diseases have genetic predisposition. 一些疾病有遗传倾向。 | pre->提前，disposition->性情/排列，predisposition->预先排好的倾向（cf. dispose处置，disposition性情）

quiz.question: _____ exposition
quiz.explanation: 展览会, 贸易展览会. Vendors gathered at the trade exposition. 供应商聚集在贸易展览会。 | ex->向外，pos->放置，exposition->向外展示放置=展览/阐述（cf. expose暴露，deposit存放，impose施加）

quiz.question: partition ____
quiz.explanation: 隔断, 隔断墙. They installed a partition wall in the office. 他们在办公室安装了隔断墙。 | part->部分（拉丁pars），partition->分成部分=隔断/划分（cf. part部分，department部门，compartment隔间）

quiz.question: ___ superstition
quiz.explanation: 迷信, 古老的迷信. The villagers clung to old superstition. 村民坚持古老的迷信。 | super->凌驾，stit->站立（拉丁stare），superstition->凌驾于理性之上的信仰=迷信（cf. supersede取代，substitute替代）

quiz.question: ____ __ fruition
quiz.explanation: 实现, 实现. The plans finally come to fruition. 计划终于实现了。 | frui->享用（拉丁frui->享受果实），fruition->结出果实=实现（cf. fruit水果，enjoy享受）

quiz.question: ______ intuition
quiz.explanation: 直觉, 女性直觉. She relied on her female intuition. 她依靠她的女性直觉。 | in->向内，tuit->看护/注视（拉丁tueri），intuition->向内注视=直觉（cf. tutor导师，tutelage监护）
